name: Amish-Style Chicken and Corn Soup
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine the stewing hen with chicken stock, onions, carrots, celery, and saffron threads in a large pot.
2. Bring to a simmer and cook for about 1 hour, skimming the surface as necessary.
3. Remove the stewing hen and let it cool before picking out the meat from the bones. Cut into pieces.
4. Strain the broth through a fine sieve and discard solids.
5. Add corn, celery, parsley, and cooked noodles to the strained broth.
6. Return to a simmer and serve immediately.
(Note: The soup can be made up to this point in advance. Refrigerate or freeze in labeled containers for 2-3 days. To use, defrost, remove any congealed fat, return the liquid to a boil, and add the meat before serving.)
